primary_sources.title: Public Prosecutor v Mohd Najib bin Abdul Razak, 1MDB-Tanore principal trial verdict, Kuala Lumpur High Court 26 December 2025
1MDB charge sheet — 4 abuse-of-power charges and 21 money-laundering charges totalling RM2.27 billion
Malaysiakini, The Edge, Free Malaysia Today coverage of 26 December 2025 verdict and immediate appeal filing

charges: Section 23 MACC Act 2009 — abuse of position (4 charges)
Section 4(1)(b) Anti-Money Laundering, Anti-Terrorism Financing and Proceeds of Unlawful Activities Act 2001 — money laundering (21 charges)
sentence_summary: Convicted on all 25 charges. 15 years imprisonment (before any pardon arithmetic). RM11.3879 billion fine (5× gratification on the 4 abuse-of-power charges). Court rejected four Saudi donation letters as forgeries. Appeal filed within days.
notes: The 1MDB-Tanore principal trial verdict — Najib's largest single criminal case. Total principal: RM2.27 billion. Distinct from the earlier SRC International conviction (2020, upheld 2022). Sets the stage for the appeal cycle through 2026-2027 and any subsequent pardon-arithmetic events. T4A issue 1944 documents the structural finding that political-financing reform remains unpassed despite the conviction.
